content.claim: Removal of 6/8-year gimnázium tracks without concurrent structural reforms to lower secondary education (addressing transition disruption at age 10 and parental distrust of state upper-primary schools) risks displacement of sorting behavior into alternative channels (private schools, cross-municipal enrollment, church-school conversions) rather than reduction of stratification, according to commentary by a Budapest school-reform figure.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: This is an inference/argument from one commentator, not an empirical finding with comparative evidence
No historical or comparative cases provided documenting displacement effects from track removal in other systems
The claim depends on unstated assumptions about parental behavior and demand drivers that are not validated in sources reviewed
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
